summary: The reproduction bundle pins Traefik v2.11.48 and verifies the missing ForwardAuth size bound by comparing the shipped unlimited path against a bounded configuration. The host in this session lacked Docker, so ./reproduction/run.sh failed during environment preflight even though the bundled helper logic was validated directly with Go.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction bundle should print UNBOUNDED_FORWARD_AUTH_CONFIRMED after showing that the default ForwardAuth path returns the full 8 MiB auth-response body while a bounded maxResponseBodySize=1024 configuration rejects the same large body without forwarding it.
actual_observed_result: Executing ./reproduction/run.sh in this session failed before container execution because the host did not have a Docker CLI/daemon available. Separately, the same helper code was executed directly with Go against both the local source snapshot and upstream Traefik v2.11.48 and emitted UNBOUNDED_FORWARD_AUTH_CONFIRMED.
verification_method: The helper runs two repository-grounded ForwardAuth cases against the pinned target source tree: an unbounded default case and a bounded control case. Verification succeeds only if the unbounded case forwards the full 8 MiB auth body and the bounded case returns 401 with no large forwarded body, then emits the exact proof string UNBOUNDED_FORWARD_AUTH_CONFIRMED.
evidence_produced_by_run: run.sh preflight failed because docker was not installed or not available on PATH on the host used for this session.
limitations_or_blockers: The current session environment does not provide a Docker CLI/daemon, so the required Docker-based run.sh workflow could not be completed end to end here.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
